name: Trail run to the top of Los Obispos (PR-MU 105)
name_original: Trail run hasta la cima de Los Obispos (PR-MU 105)
region_intro: Jogging around Moratalla offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by mountainous terrain, extensive woodlands, and clear rivers. The region provides routes with significant elevation changes, ideal for trail running, alongside shaded paths through pine and oak forests. Features like the Bolvonegro Strait, carved by the Alhárabe River, present visually interesting backdrops for runs. This varied terrain ensures a range of jogging experiences. Best jogging routes around Moratalla The most popular jogging route is Trail run to the top of Los Obispos (PR-MU 105), a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) difficult trail that takes about 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, offering challenging ascents and expansive views. Another top favourite among local runners is Moratalla – El Buitre Summit loop from Moratalla, a difficult 13.9 miles (22.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Local runners also love the Somogil Baths loop from Camping La Puerta, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 43 minutes. Jogging around Moratalla is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive woodlands, and clear rivers, with routes often featuring natural pools and rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Moratalla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 1 review. More than 240 runners have used komoot to explore Moratalla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running trails can I expect in Moratalla?
A: Moratalla offers a diverse range of running trails, characterized by its mountainous terrain, extensive woodlands of pine, holm, and oak, and clear rivers like the Alhárabe and Benamor. You'll find routes with significant elevation changes ideal for challenging trail runs, as well as shaded paths through forests and scenic routes along riverbanks.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes in Moratalla?
A: While many routes in Moratalla are challenging, there are options for moderate difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somogil Baths loop from Camping La Puerta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.2-mile (6.7 km) trail that follows riverine landscapes, often completed in about 43 minutes. This route offers a less strenuous option compared to the more difficult mountain trails.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Moratalla?
A: Moratalla's routes are rich with natural beauty. You can encounter spectacular gorges like the Bolvonegro Strait, carved by the Alhárabe River, showcasing unique rock formations. Many trails lead to natural pools such as the Pozas de Somogil, or scenic waterfalls like the Hondares Stream Waterfall (Poza de las Tortugas). Mountainous routes often provide stunning panoramic views, including from Revolcadores Peak.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the running routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural landscapes, Moratalla offers glimpses into ancient history. The region is home to one of Murcia's largest collections of Mediterranean rock art, with sites like El Calar de la Santa and the Caña shelters, which are World Heritage sites. The Bolvonegro Strait itself reveals ancient marine shells and geological strata, adding a historical dimension to your run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Moratalla?
A: Spring and autumn are considered the ideal seasons for outdoor activities like jogging in Moratalla. During these times, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable conditions. Spring brings fruit blossoms, while autumn offers beautiful lavender fields, enhancing the scenic beauty of your runs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Moratalla?
A: Yes, Moratalla features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – El Buitre Summit loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 13.9-mile (22.4 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somogil Baths loop from Camping La Puerta&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 4.2-mile (6.7 km) route.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Moratalla?
A: The running trails in Moratalla are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents to serene riverine paths, and the opportunity to explore unique natural features like gorges and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced trail runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Moratalla offers demanding long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – El Buitre Summit loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.9-mile (22.4 km) path with nearly 930 meters of elevation gain, providing a significant endurance test through mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Many of Moratalla's trails, especially those in the mountainous areas, are known for their stunning panoramic vistas. Routes that ascend to peaks like Revolcadores offer expansive views of the surrounding landscape. The House of Christ Sanctuary also provides a natural balcony with wonderful views of the entire Sierra de Moratalla.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the more difficult running routes?
A: The more difficult running routes in Moratalla typically feature rugged, mountainous terrain with significant elevation changes. You can expect challenging ascents and descents, often through rocky paths and forest trails. These routes are ideal for vigorous jogging and trail running, requiring good physical condition and sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any specific gorges or canyons I can explore while running?
A: Yes, the region is home to several impressive gorges. The Bolvonegro Strait, carved by the Alhárabe River, is a spectacular gorge with beautiful sedimentary rock formations, providing a visually interesting backdrop for a run. Other notable gorges include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5434950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hondón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any castles or historical sites near the running trails?
A: Yes, Moratalla and its surroundings are dotted with historical sites. You can find castles such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Caravaca de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templar Tower&lt;/a&gt;. These historical landmarks offer interesting points to explore or view during your running adventures.
tips: The Pico de los Obispos is the highest in the Province of Murcia. With an altitude of 2014 meters above sea level, you will have amazing views in all directions. You can get here by the Revolcadores Path starting from Inazares. Remember to wear suitable footwear and plenty of water, especially if you go in summer.
